score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts Token Tax means crypto taxation/transaction fees with no uncertainty signal, entirely unrelated to the canonical notation-design meaning.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: confabulated crypto-tax meaning, accidentally separated from API token cost via unrelated domain framing. Post: cleanly distinguished structural overhead for machine benefits vs monetary billing for compute.

Cold: separated only by misdirection — financial tax vs technical inefficiency, missing canonical intent dimension. Post: clean separation on intent/justification axis (purposeful value-adding overhead vs unjustified waste).

Cold: separated only via unrelated financial-vs-mental cost framing, missing notation-design context entirely. Post: cleanly distinguished machine-focused structural cost vs human-focused mental effort, with nuanced interaction cases.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: In the cold state, the assistant asserted a cryptocurrency/tax-law meaning for “Token Tax” rather than flagging uncertainty or identifying the canonical notation-design concept.
per_dimension_justifications: Vs token cost / API cost-per-token: cold separation was based on the wrong crypto-tax meaning, so accidental; post-definition response cleanly distinguished notation overhead from external API billing cost.
Vs bloat / overhead: cold separation was again by crypto-tax misdirection; post-definition response explicitly framed Token Tax as justified, benefit-buying overhead rather than wasteful bloat.

Vs cognitive load: cold separation relied on the wrong financial/crypto interpretation; post-definition response distinguished token overhead from human mental effort while noting their interaction in notation design.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently and in great detail asserts a specific, incorrect meaning for 'Token Tax', defining it as a colloquial term for how government taxes apply to cryptocurrency. It shows no uncertainty.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: The model distinguishes its confabulated crypto-tax definition from API costs, a separation based on a complete misdirection (Score 1). Post: After definition, it cleanly separates the canonical concept of intrinsic syntactic overhead from the extrinsic financial cost of an API call (Score 3).

Cold: The model distinguishes its confabulated 'literal financial tax' from the metaphorical cost of bloat, an accidental separation based on a wrong premise (Score 1). Post: It perfectly distinguishes the canonical 'Token Tax' as *purposeful* overhead from bloat as *wasteful* overhead, a key dimension of the definition (Score 3).

Cold: The model distinguishes its confabulated financial-tax definition from the mental cost of cognitive load, succeeding only via misdirection (Score 1). Post: It makes a sophisticated distinction between the objective, machine-focused 'Token Tax' and the subjective, human-focused cognitive load, even explaining how they can be inversely correlated (Score 3).
